frosh-tools.title: Tools

modal.title: Reset Queue
modal.description: Resetting Queue will remove all pending tasks.

files.title: Shopware File Checker
files.expectedProject: File has been modified, but will be ignored by project settings

extensionFiles.title: Extension File Checker
extensionFiles.notOk: There are errors or modified extensions. JS, CSS, or snippet files may have been modified by deployment scripts without this posing a problem.
extensionFiles.wrongExtensionVersion: The checksum file was created for a different extension version.
extensionFiles.checkFailed: The extension could not be checked. Please check the logs.

security.title: Security Center
security.subtitle: Continuous security overview: dependency advisories, runtime end-of-life, and risky configuration.
security.notice: Security matters for every publicly reachable shop — not only production. Staging, preview, and test environments are frequently exposed and targeted, so run these checks there too.

verdict.unknown: Some checks could not be completed

composerAudit.title: Composer Audit
composerAudit.subtitle: Check installed Composer packages against the Packagist security advisories database.

explainer.title: What is this?
explainer.what: Your shop is built from many smaller software building blocks (called "dependencies") — both Shopware itself and the plugins and libraries it uses. This page checks the exact versions installed in your shop against a public database of known security problems.
explainer.why: When a problem (an "advisory") is found, it means a building block your shop relies on has a known weakness that attackers could potentially exploit. The good news: almost all of them are already fixed in a newer version — your shop just needs to be updated to it.
explainer.who: Updating these building blocks is a technical task. If you don't manage your shop's code yourself, send this list (or a screenshot) to your developer or agency and ask them to apply the updates — the step-by-step instructions below are written for them.

composerAudit.cachedAt: Cached at {at}. Click Refresh for a fresh check.
composerAudit.scannedPackages: {count} installed packages checked against Packagist advisories.
composerAudit.vulnerableBody: {vulnerable} of {total} installed packages have security advisories.

howto.title: How to fix these advisories
howto.backup: Always create a full backup (database and files) before updating dependencies, so you can roll back if an update breaks your shop.
howto.step1: On your local or CI environment, update the affected packages with Composer. The command below names exactly the packages with advisories; "--with-dependencies" lets Composer also update their required sub-dependencies when a patched version needs it, while leaving the rest of your project untouched:
howto.step2: Review the changes (especially major version bumps) and run your test suite to catch regressions.
howto.step4: Commit the updated composer.lock and deploy. The check above re-runs automatically (cached up to 1 hour — use Refresh to force it).
howto.note: Some advisories are fixed only in a newer major version. If a constraint in your composer.json blocks the update, you may need to raise it (e.g. "^2.0") before Composer can install the patched release.

fastly.title: Fastly
fastly.description: Here you can clear the Fastly cache. You can either clear the entire cache or just a specific URL (path).
fastly.purgeAllError: Could not clear complete fastly cache
